content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Shannon L. Davis 
2617 "G" Street, #15 
Sacramento, CA 
95816 
Dear Hr. Davis: 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/Legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
322·5901 
322-6<441 
August 19, 1980 
rtC: 
Upinion Request 
No. 80-006 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
I am in receipt of your reYY7st for an opinion pursuant 
to Government Code Section 83114-
regarding the propriety 
of a letter from City Councilman John Roberts to the Department 
of Alcoholic Beverage Control (ABCD) in opposition to the 
grant of a liquor license at 1401 "G" Street. 
Because, 
according to your letter, Councilman Roberts owns a bar at 
2712 "J" Street, you are seeking an opinion on the question 
whether the letter to the ABCD ostensibly written in Mr. Robert's 
capacity as a councilman represents the use of official 
position to influence a governmental decision in which he is 
financially interested. 
Section 83114 provides that "any ~erson may request the 
Commission to issue an opinion with respect to his duties 
under" the Pol i tical Reform Ac t. 
Under tne Act -;-Opinions 
pursuant to Section 83114 are issued for the guidance of the 
official whose duties are in question; they are not issued 
in response to complaints about official action. 
Complaints 
about the propriety of specific actions are properly raised 
either pursuant to Section 83115 or by the initiation of 
legal action pursuant to Sections 91001 and 91U~3. 
If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion reyuest, 
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. 
If you have any questions, please contact Thomas M. Sobel, 
Chief of the Commission's Legal Division. 
Sincerely, 
':'ed Prim 
Executive Director 
TP:TMS:plh 
II All statutory references are to the Government Code 
unless otherwise noted.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Councilman's letter to the Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control opposing a liquor license constitutes improper use of official position to influence a governmental decision in which he has a financial interest, given that he owns a competing bar.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue an opinion because Section 83114 opinions are issued only for guidance of officials whose own duties are in question, not in response to complaints about official action. Complaints about specific official actions should be raised through Section 83115 procedures or legal action under Sections 91001 and 91003.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter, not a traditional advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the legal question is described in the body text. The document denies the opinion request and explains the proper procedural channels for raising the complaint.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a City Councilman's letter to the Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control opposing a liquor license constitutes improper use of official position to influence a governmental decision in which he has a financial interest, given that he owns a competing bar.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to issue an opinion because Section 83114 opinions are issued only for guidance of officials whose own duties are in question, not in response to complaints about official action. Complaints about specific official actions should be raised through Section 83115 procedures or legal action under Sections 91001 and 91003.

Because, according to your letter, Councilman Roberts owns a bar at 2712 "J" Street, you are seeking an opinion on the question whether the letter to the ABCD ostensibly written in Mr. Robert's capacity as a councilman represents the use of official position to influence a governmental decision in which he is financially interested. embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue an opinion regarding whether a City Councilman's opposition letter to the Department of Alcoholic Beverage Control regarding a liquor license near his own bar constituted improper use of official position, finding that such complaints must be addressed through enforcement procedures rather than advisory opinions.
